column2: starting Dagger 0.2 (Europa) we introduced a brand new API which removed this capability temporarily until we come up with the [right UX](https://github.com/dagger/dagger/issues/1351) to bring this back. Until the API is ready, you can follow this guide which shows different **temporary** alternatives of parsing dagger outputs.

+This is very useful to get immediate feedback on an action's results.

-After performing `dagger do test`, a new file named `output.txt` will be present in the current working directory with the output of the `bash.#Run` action.
+You can silence the `info` logs by raising the log level (or redirecting _stderr_ somewhere else):
+➜ dagger -l error --output-format json do push | jq '.result'
+You can also save the output to a file using the `--output` flag. Let's do it in _yaml_ this time:

In this case, Netlify's action is returning the deployment `url`, `deployUrl` and `logsUrl`. On this case, we can leverage on CUE's [default integrations](https://cuelang.org/docs/integrations/) and marshal all the values into a single `json` or `yaml` file.
-Here's an example on how to marshal multiple output values:
+You're not limited to the outputs of an action because you can make your own in a wrapper action:

+Since you can only output simple values, you may find the need for a solution where you can output more complex types such as structs and lists. As showcased in the [interacting with the client](../core-concepts/1203-client.md) docs, Dagger has the ability to write into the client filesystem through the `client` API.

+Using this capability we can then have full control of what to output. The downsides are that these won't print to the console (only to a file), and you won't be able to pipe directly from the `dagger` command.
+Let's leverage CUE's [default integrations](https://cuelang.org/docs/integrations/) and marshal a more complex value into a single `json` or `yaml` file.

- documents protocol file as well",2825e751fbb0cedd595c9155e8b6423ff917c5c8,docs,https://github.com/camunda/zeebe/commit/2825e751fbb0cedd595c9155e8b6423ff917c5c8,docs,"introduce gRPC API documentation
- documents protocol file as well","diff --git a/docs/src/SUMMARY.md b/docs/src/SUMMARY.md
+Zeebe clients connect to brokers via a stateless gateway. For the communication
+between client and gateway gRPC is used. The communication protocol is defined using
+Protocol Buffers v3 (proto3), and you can find it in the
+gRPC was first developed by Google and is now an open-source project and part of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation.
+If you’re new to gRPC, the [“What is gRPC”](https://grpc.io/docs/guides/index.html) page on the project website provides a good introduction to it.
+gRPC has many nice features that make it a good fit for Zeebe. It:
+  - supports bi-directional streaming for opening a persistent connection and sending or receiving a stream of messages between client and server
+  - uses the common http2 protocol by default
+  - uses Protocol Buffers as an interface definition and data serialization mechanism–specifically, Zeebe uses proto3, which supports easy client generation in ten different programming languages
+At the moment, Zeebe officially supports two gRPC clients: one in [Java](java-client/README.md), and one in [Golang](go-client/README.md).
+If Zeebe does not provide an officially-supported client in your target language, you can read the official [Quick Start](https://grpc.io/docs/quickstart/) page to find out how
+You can find a list of existing clients in the [Awesome Zeebe repository](https://github.com/zeebe-io/awesome-zeebe#clients).
+Additionally, a [blog post](https://zeebe.io/blog/2018/11/grpc-generating-a-zeebe-python-client/) was published with a short tutorial on how to write a new client from scratch in Python.
+The gRPC API for Zeebe is exposed through the gateway, which acts as a proxy
+for the broker. Generally, this means that the client executes an remote call on the gateway,
+which is then translated to special binary protocol that the gateway uses to
+As a result of this proxying, any errors which occur between the gateway and the broker
+*for which the client is not at fault* (e.g. the gateway cannot deserialize the broker response,
+the broker is unavailable, etc.) are reported to the client as internal errors
+using the `GRPC_STATUS_INTERNAL` code. One exception to this is if the gateway itself is in
+an invalid state (e.g. out of memory), at which point it will return `GRPC_STATUS_UNAVAILABLE`.
+This behavior applies to every single possible RPC
column2: in these cases, it is possible that retrying

column1: +would succeed, but it is recommended to do so with an appropriate retry policy
+(e.g. a combination of exponential backoff or jitter wrapped in a circuit breaker).

+errors which are a result of request processing logic, and not serialization, network, or
+> As the gRPC server/client is based on generated code, keep in mind that
+any call made to the server can return errors as described by the spec
+The Zeebe gRPC API is exposed through a single gateway service.

+of jobs and streams them back to the client as they are activated.

When arguments are used, haves are reset every round in stateless protocols, while
everything else is repeated. However, this also means that previously confirmed common
commits aren't repeated unless this is specifically implemented by the user of `Arguments`.
That caller can now easily determine if negotiations have to be compensated for.

-Router 主要用来描述请求 URL 和具体承担执行动作的 Controller 的对应关系，
-框架约定了 `app/router.js` 文件用于统一所有路由规则。

+Router is mainly used to describe the relationship between the request URL and the Controller that processes the request eventually. All routing rules are unified in the `app/router.js` file by the framework.
-通过统一的配置，我们可以避免路由规则逻辑散落在多个地方，从而出现未知的冲突，集中在一起我们可以更方便的来查看全局的路由规则。
+By unifying routing rules, routing logics are free from scatter that may cause unkown conflicts and it will be easier for us to check global routing rules.
-Beidou框架提供了两种路由方式供用户选择，其一是自动路由，其二是配置路由
+We provide 2 ways to use router. One is auto router, another one is config router.
-每一个在/client目录下的页面都会被自动路由，举个例子， `/client/page-a.jsx` 页面可以无需配置任何路由，访问`http://localhost:6001/page-a`该页面就会被自动渲染，映射关系如下：
+Every page in /client folder will be routed automatically.
-| 客户端页面          | 路由 | 备注           |
-注. 支持热加载, 比如动态添加`a.jsx`页面，无需重启应用, 直接访问`http://localhost:6001/a`即可.
+PS. hot-loading is supported, suppose that `a.jsx` was added dynamically after the application started, then `http://localhost:6001/a` is available without restarting the application.
-如果自动路由无法满足项目需要，请参考下面的步骤配置路由，配置路由会和自动路由同时生效，且优先级高于自动路由。
+If auto router can't meet your needs, please follow the next steps to use config router.
-- `app/router.js` 里面定义 URL 路由规则
+- Define the routing rule in `app/router.js` file
+This simplest Router is done by now, when users do the request `GET /user/123`, the info function in `user.js` will be invoked.
-这样就完成了一个最简单的 Router 定义，当用户执行 `GET /user/123`，`user.js` 这个里面的 info 方法就会执行。
-下面是路由的完整定义，参数可以根据场景的不同，自由选择：
+Below is the complete definition of router, parameters can be determined depending on different scenes.
-路由完整定义主要包括5个主要部分:
-- verb - 用户触发动作，支持 get，post 等所有 HTTP 方法，后面会通过示例详细说明。
-  * router.del - 由于 delete 是一个保留字，所以提供了一个 delete 方法的别名。
-  * router.redirect - 可以对 URL 进行重定向处理，比如我们最经常使用的可以把用户访问的根目录路由到某个主页。

feat(engine): generate terminate end event compatible execution steps part 1

The random execution tests don't know the concept of flow scopes. This makes it challenging to generate a correct execution path for terminate end events, as they terminate a specific flow scope. Processing should continue as normal once the flow scope has been terminated.

Whilst we don't have flow scopes, we do have execution path segments. These segments don't map 1 to 1 to flow scopes. However, since every flow scope starts a new segment we can use these segments to get the desired behavior.

Each segment must keep track whether is has reached a terminate end event. If this is the case that means that we don't expect any further execution steps. We can isolate this behavior in a single location, during the appending of one segment to another segment.

In order to differentiate between flow scopes a new append method has been added which takes the boolean `changesFlowScope` as a parameter. Blockbuilder where the flow scope changes (e.g. SubProcessBlockBuilder) can use this to indicate that even though a terminate end event has been reached. Execution steps after this specific segment still need to added to complete the process.

When a segment is appended to a different segment and the flow scope does not change we can use the segment that should be appended to identify whether new segment can still be added to the current segment. If passed segment has reached a terminate end event and the flow scope has not been changed it is guaranteed that the current segment is in the same flow scope has the previous segment and thus has also reached the terminate end event.",40597fb4de41c7194eb99479a914db70da7909ea,feat,https://github.com/camunda/zeebe/commit/40597fb4de41c7194eb99479a914db70da7909ea,feat,"generate terminate end event compatible execution steps part 1

drastically increased code size. Some of the macros could be turned
into functions, while others could be removed entirely.
Examples were removing arg_post_processing! which did things like
checked overrides, requirements, groups, etc. This would happen
after every argument was parsed. This macro also had several other
macros inside it, and would expand to several tens or hundreds of
Then add that due to borrowck and branch issues, this macro may be

each of these uses expanded into TONS of code (just like agressive
This commit primarily removes those arg_post_processing! calls and
breaks up the functionality into two types. The first must happen at
ever new argument (not new value, but new argument). This is pretty
cheap. The next type was moved to the end of parsing validation section
which is more expensive, but only happens once.
